pattern: Expanding every substring start rightward while it stays all-vowel and tracking the distinct vowels seen counts, in one pass per start, exactly the windows that contain all five vowels.
hints: A qualifying substring can never contain a consonant, so stop expanding the moment you hit one.
For each starting index, grow the end index while every character stays a vowel, keeping a set of which of the five vowels have appeared so far.
Every time that set reaches all five vowels, the current window counts -- and it stays counted even as you keep growing further, since a longer all-vowel window is still valid.

edge_inputs.description: exactly the five vowels in order, one qualifying window
the five vowels repeated back to back, overlapping windows
single character, far short of all five vowels
each vowel repeated several times before the next appears
no vowels anywhere in the string
four of the five vowels repeated but 'u' never appears
max length string made of the five vowels repeated

stress.description: random letters biased toward vowels at the max string length
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 100))
    alphabet = 'aeiou' * 2 + 'bcdfghjklmnpqrstvwxyz'
    return [''.join(rng.choice(alphabet) for _ in range(n))]
